narration: Dynamic programming saves smaller answers so bigger answers do not have to redo the same work.
callout: The big idea is solve once, save, and reuse.

narration: Base cases are the first tiny answers we already know for sure.
decision: Without base cases, the DP table has nowhere to start.

predictionPrompt.prompt: What is the job of a base case?

choices.label: Give the first sure answers
choices.feedback: Correct. Base cases are the small known answers that let the rest grow.

choices.label: Only describe the final answer
choices.feedback: Not this one. Base cases are the beginning of the build.

narration: Overlapping subproblems means the same smaller question shows up more than once.
callout: Saving F(3) once keeps us from solving it again and again.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is repeated smaller work a clue for dynamic programming?
